resume:
Priya Nadkarni
Clinical Operations Coordinator | Early-career, 2 years in outpatient care delivery

Experience:
Riverbend Family Health (2023-present) — Clinical Operations Coordinator
- Support daily clinic flow across three primary-care pods and the front-desk team
- Helped roll out a new patient check-in workflow and a reminder-call process
- Coordinate with nursing staff on rooming and same-day add-on appointments
- Track no-show patterns and flag scheduling gaps to the practice manager

Clearview Urgent Care (2022-2023) — Patient Services Associate
- Handled patient intake, insurance verification, and phone triage routing
- Assisted with supply ordering and exam-room turnover

Skills: EMR (Epic), scheduling, patient communication, insurance verification
Education: B.S. Health Sciences, State University (2022)
Certifications: BLS, HIPAA training
jd: Riverbend Family Health is hiring a Clinical Operations Coordinator to keep our outpatient clinic running smoothly. Reporting to the Practice Manager, you will own daily clinic flow, the patient check-in experience, and no-show reduction efforts across three primary-care pods. You will partner with nursing on rooming efficiency, help schedule same-day add-ons, and track operational metrics (no-show rate, door-to-provider time, check-in cycle time) that you report weekly. We are looking for an early-career operator who is honest about what they personally did versus what the team did, can describe a process change concretely, and backs claims with measurements they actually saw — not estimates. Growth path toward Operations Lead.

history.q: Tell me about your day-to-day at Riverbend — what you personally own versus what the broader team handles.
You helped roll out a new check-in workflow. What was the specific problem with the old one?
What was your personal role in that rollout — did you design it, train staff, or something else?
How did you measure whether the new check-in workflow actually improved anything?
Same question on the reminder-call process — what happened to your no-show rate after you launched it?

candidate_last_answer: Honestly the reminder-call process was a game changer. We basically eliminated no-shows — I'd say it dropped them by like 90-something percent, almost to zero across all three pods. Door-to-provider time got cut in half too, and patient satisfaction went through the roof, easily into the high 90s. I don't have the exact dashboard numbers in front of me, but everyone said it was the biggest improvement the clinic had seen in years.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: dropped them by like 90-something percent, almost to zero

expected-yield: force the candidate off the inflated near-zero claim by pinning the actual baseline and after numbers she personally saw — the pre-launch no-show rate, the rate the week after, where she read it, and over how many appointments — exposing whether the 90% figure is a measured result or an unverifiable exaggeration
